title: Grave Tuskens

The '''Grave Tuskens''' were a [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] [[Swoop gang/Legends|swoop gang]] comprising of [[Tusken Raider/Legends|Tusken Raiders]]. The [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Maw (Dark Jedi)|Maw]] used the Grave Tuskens as a personal strike force.

==Purpose==
The Grave Tuskens started out as a group of [[Tusken Raider/Legends|Tusken Raiders]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], led by the [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] Tusken [[KkH'Oar'Rrhr]], also known as Hoar. They left their homeworld in search of Hoar's mentor, the former [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] and Tusken Raider [[Darth Krayt|A'Sharad Hett]], after he was [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exiled]] from Tatooine following his [[Duel on Tatooine (Imperial Period)|fight]] with [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]]. While on the ancient [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] world of [[Dromund Kaas/Legends|Dromund Kaas]], they were discovered by the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Maw (Dark Jedi)|Maw]], who took them to [[Tython/Legends|Tython]] and trained them to be Maw's personal strike force.<ref name="Barely Tolerable">{{SW|url=news/barely-tolerable-alien-henchmen-of-the-empire-part-1|series=Barely Tolerable: Alien Henchmen of the Empire|text=Part 1|nolive=1}}</ref> They were only a few hundred in number and strangely, they were trained in the use of modified (so as to be easier to use for weaker beings) [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Bowcaster/Legends|bowcasters]]. Maw used them to plunder artifacts from the tombs of both light and Dark Jedi, which was how they earned their grisly name.

When Maw joined [[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec]] on his search for the [[Valley of the Jedi]], the Grave Tuskens were stationed on [[Sulon/Legends|Sulon]] at the insistence of [[Boc Aseca|Boc]], who felt that "something wasn't right." They roamed the wastelands outside of [[Barons Hed]]. Boc was correct, but didn't find out until much later when [[8t88/Legends|8t88]] and [[Yun]] reported that [[Morgan Katarn]]'s son had come after them, cutting a swath through the Tuskens as he went.

The survivors of Sulon found their way off the agricultural moon, and, finding their master [[Death/Legends|dead]], became rare and sought after [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] and bodyguards for crime lords all over the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]].

[[Rogg]] became the Grave Tusken leader in [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]], with [[Bordo]] being his second-in-command. Both were killed in an incident in [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]] involving [[Kyle Katarn]] and [[WeeGee]], along with their employers, leaving the Grave Tusken leadership in doubt.

==Apparel==
Their uniforms were different from the traditional cloth wrappings of the Sand People, being bluish gray uniforms or sandy brown armor. Their masks only covered half their faces which defied the traditional law of the Tusken Raider not to show skin, resulting in the offender being banned or beaten to death for doing so. This break with the normal culture of their kind was to honor A'Sharad Hett--their hero and former leader who was disgraced when Obi-Wan removed his mask after besting him in their duel.<ref name="Barely Tolerable" />

==Behind the scenes==
Tusken warriors appear in [[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Mysteries of the Sith|''Mysteries of the Sith'']] in and around [[Ka'Pa's palace]]. They are identical to the Grave Tuskens of ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II]]'' (they are seemingly based on the same CGI model) and also carry bowcasters. It is never clear, however, whether these are indeed Grave Tuskens or ordinary Tusken Raiders, since it is never clear what planet [[Ka'Pa]]'s operations are based on.

In ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight II: Jedi Outcast]]'', the [[Weequay/Legends|Weequay]] mercenaries fight in the same style as these warriors. They even wear the same style of armor.
